title: Can be a great job for while, but not sustainable.
body: Can be a great job for while, but not sustainable.
pros: Competitive wages, opportunity for growth, nice company incentives (parties, gifts, company picnic), and a free meal per shift.
cons: The quality of your shift will depend on which manager is leading, and can be completely unpredictable. Depending on your store location, you can have practically no business or never-ending orders all day. The work is tiring, especially the grill, and you're expected to just be able to handle it, and are often not given your break on time. The company doesn't care about work-life balance and expects you to regularly overexert yourself, or else you're not "a team player". Higher management (District and Regional Managers) will only like you if you're an In-N-Out drone or a professional bootlicker. A lot of pressure is put on every associate to be practically perfect; more often than not, I see people get ridiculed for making a mistake.

title: Great Company
pros: This company is very much customer service oriented. From the top to the bottom the main goal is take care of both internal and external customers. There is lots of room to grow and invest your time into a career that would apply to the hospitality and restaurant industry. The time spent there is well worth in and the return you receive from being an exemplary Associate does not go unnoticed in this company. Everyone in the company is nice and helpful; they all want each other to succeed which is nice especially in this type of job market.
cons: There isn't much negatives. This company does do a great job of taking care of their Associates and protecting them at the same time. There are some internal bureaucratic events that may occur, but that's everywhere. Opportunity is all out there at this company and they reward those who stick with them even if there is a longer timeline associated with it.

title: Decent pay very strict itâs goofy

pros: Pays well they like to move people up and there are many hours to work night or day
cons: Weird company rules that go down to the details of bras and what girls can style makeup in what manner. It felt like highschool with a bit of a cult undertones. When i resigned I put a two week notice and was told I would never get rehired because I had a job lined up in a different career field. Iâm not sure if thatâs an actual rule or if it was a scare tactic but very odd regardless. Also so often do we almost get ran over in the parking lot nobody notices our burger uniform as something to not run over.
role: Former employee - In N Out Associate level 4

title: Thoughts after working here for 2 years
body: Thoughts after working here for 2 years
cons: Really hard to get full time Late policy is incredibly strict. Had multiple write ups while I was there for being 1-5 min late. The computers flag you INSTANTLY SUPER militant. Your uniform has to be perfect, the product has to be perfect, your customer service has to be perfect. YOU have to be perfect No dyed hair, men also have to be kept clean shaven & have to have short hair. No visible tattoos, you just have to cover them though it's not a huge deal. WHITE uniforms, super hard to keep clean, highly recommend oxiclean & SHOUT stain remover. The work pants they give you suck and will not last, recommend Dickies 874 pants in white. Gets very cliquey very fast especially in the LA area. Management is competitive as hell, since there's only a certain number of stores, there's only a certain number of management positions. So it can be hard to break in and favoritism can screw you over Because of stated cliquiness and favoritism, harassment claims against management are difficult...

title: Great First Job
pros: In-N-Out Burger provided an outstanding foundation for my professional growth, instilling exceptional customer service skills and valuable workplace habits. The companyâs willingness to hire high school students with no prior experience creates a welcoming environment for young workers, and their comprehensive training and development programs ensure employees are well-prepared to excel in future roles. I am grateful to have had In-N-Out as my first job, and I highly recommend it to anyone seeking a reputable and reliable employer. Whether youâre looking for an impressive first job to enhance your resume or opportunities to advance within the company, In-N-Out offers both. Additionally, the positive, team-oriented culture makes it a friendly and enjoyable place to work. To this day, I continue to use the valuable skills and work ethic that In-N-Out Burger instilled in me, proving their training prepares employees for long-term success.
cons: The only con I experienced was that hours could be somewhat limited due to the large number of employees hired. However, this also reflects the companyâs commitment to providing opportunities for many team members.

title: Great job, enjoyable people to work with.
body: Great job, enjoyable people to work with.
pros: Some pros the job provided to me was affordable healthcare, vision, and dental insurance. Plus, it was nice to work with and see everyone every day, which added to the enjoyment of the work.
cons: Downsides from my perspective is the expectation of constant excellence. While it's great that they strive to be perfect all the time, it fosters a culture in which not being the best isn't necessarily tolerated. But then again, it all depends on your manager. I personally liked my manager.

cons: Extremely fast paced, if you work slow you wont last long. My store was one of the busiest in the country and they made sure we knew it every month. There is also a pretty strict grooming standard (no facial hair/ no arm tattoos (some exceptions)).

title: Working here during college
pros: The pay is awesome. It truly is like a sports team environment. They are great with my schedule. I work as much or as little as I want without being punished. Great place for a college student.
cons: The longer you stay here the more you see the negatives. You can have a great starting experience but a horrible experience at times. Because they are constantly moving managers around you get all types. Some can be very verbally abusive. The most important person to like you is the store manager, if they don't transfer stores until you find one that you like and they like you.

title: Proâs and Cons
pros: Itâs a good company to work for. You learn hard-worker skills. You get your limits tested if you really let them work you a lot of hours. You can be pushed a lot if you want to move up the ranks. Itâs all up to you. If youâre strong minded and donât mind being pushed to become better than itâs a good job for you. Itâs important to always have a learners mindset here, and to welcome criticism because it can help you grow.
cons: Itâs just very tiring at times and you feel like you start to lose patience with people. But thatâs why itâs important to push yourself and learn to grow a new patience beyond your own.

title: Not a bad place to work if youâre 16
body: Not a bad place to work if youâre 16
pros: Very flexible schedule. Management is very forgiving on mistakes. You learn how to work in a rush as a proper team. You build customer service skills. Pay is higher than most fast-food places.
cons: If youâre a guy, prepare to see all the girls around you level up faster that you especially when the management team is full of women. This job is only worth it if youâre trying to be a manager by climbing through all 7 lower levels. Other than that youâre overworked for what youâre being paid and the work place drama can be extremely toxic especially when itâs all mostly teenage kids working there. If you work at a smaller store, you wonât get enough hours.
